commit.message:
Fix SSE race condition with deterministic done_published_at flag (#1261)

* Initial plan

* fix(app): drain SSE event queue for late-connecting clients on finished jobs

When a client connects to the SSE events endpoint after a job has
already finished, the endpoint would previously yield only 'state'
and 'done' events, skipping any 'log', 'progress', or 'artifact'
events that were queued during the job's execution.

This fix drains the subscriber's event queue before yielding the
synthetic 'done' event, ensuring late-connecting clients receive
all events that were published during the job's lifecycle.

Fixes CI failure on main branch where
test_job_events_stream_emits_state_log_progress_artifact_done
was flaky due to a race condition between job completion and
SSE client connection timing.

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/9eae8c67-ab90-41cb-beea-99cbde490ff1

* fix(app): address code review - use atomic queue drain pattern

- Remove race-prone `while not q.empty()` check
- Use `while True` with `QueueEmpty` exception for atomic drain
- Add documentation explaining the two 'done' event paths

Add workflow-level permissions blocks to workflows that only had job-level
permissions. CodeQL's MissingActionsPermissions query requires workflow-level

Also pin several unpinned action references (checkout@v6, setup-python@v6)
to their commit SHAs to address potential UseOfKnownVulnerableAction findings.

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/af846206-8c6c-4776-88a6-48e746e50759

* fix: address Copilot review comments on PR #1261

1. tests/test_pypi_workflows.py: Fix misleading comment that said "@v7"
   but actually accepts v4+. Updated to clarify the pattern matches any
   major version and the assertion enforces v4+ minimum.

2. .github/workflows/submit-pypi.yml: Pin all remaining unpinned actions
   to commit SHAs for consistent security posture (CWE-1395):
   - actions/upload-artifact@v7 -> @bbbca2ddaa5d...# v7
   - actions/download-artifact@v8.0.1 -> @70fc10c6e5e1...# v8
   - pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish: add # v1 version comment

3. app.py: Fix SSE event race condition where job.finished_at is set
   before artifact/done events are published. Late-connecting clients
   now wait briefly (0.5s) for real events before generating synthetic
   done, preventing dropped artifact events.

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/65f345ee-8200-4aef-98f7-758df7c74608

* fix: apply deterministic fix for SSE late-connect race condition

Addresses code review feedback requesting a deterministic fix instead
of a timed wait:

1. Add `done_published_at` field to Job class to track when terminal
   events have actually been published to subscribers

2. In `_run_job()`, move artifact indexing and event publication to
   BEFORE setting finished_at/done_published_at. This ensures:
   - Late-connecting clients see done_published_at=None during indexing
   - They wait for real events instead of synthesizing prematurely

3. In `job_events()` SSE endpoint:
   - Check done_published_at (not finished_at) for synthesis decision
   - Only synthesize when done_published_at is set (events published)
   - Fall through to event loop when finished_at set but not done_published_at

4. Add regression tests:
   - test_late_connecting_client_receives_real_events_during_artifact_indexing
   - test_late_connecting_client_synthesizes_done_after_done_published_at

5. Fix setup-python regex in test_pypi_workflows.py to use \d+ instead
   of [56] for future-proofing (supports v7+ when released)

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/4b071571-efaa-43e9-900f-6a481104f6fd

* fix: set done_published_at in tests to prevent SSE endpoint hangs

Tests that create finished jobs and test SSE endpoints were hanging
because they set finished_at but not done_published_at. The SSE
endpoint now relies on done_published_at to determine when it's safe
to synthesize a done event. Without it, the endpoint falls through
to the event loop and waits forever for events that never come.

Fixed tests:
- test_v1_routes_enforce_api_key_for_reads_and_events
- test_archive_gate_pipeline_submission_returns_job_envelope
- test_job_events_stream_emits_state_log_progress_artifact_done

Exclude gitignored files from Lambda layer and source code bundles (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: exclude gitignored files from lambda layer and code bundles

- Add gitignore_exclude_patterns() utility to bundler.py that reads the
  root .gitignore and returns all non-comment, non-blank patterns
- DepsBundler.__init__ now stores patterns; try_bundle passes them to
  shutil.copytree(ignore=...) so __pycache__, *.pyc, .venv/, etc. are
  excluded when copying workspace packages into the layer
- hello_stack.py passes exclude=gitignore_exclude_patterns() with
  ignore_mode=IgnoreMode.GIT to Code.from_asset for the lambda handler,
  keeping gitignored artefacts out of the deployment zip

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/klemjul/python-aws-monorepo-boilerplate/sessions/bbd1571f-8fa5-4142-b027-9555f94766b0

* fix: apply ignore callback at top level of workspace src copy

Replace the manual entry-by-entry loop with a single shutil.copytree call
on src_dir directly, so the ignore callback (built from .gitignore patterns)
is applied at every level including the top level of src/. The old loop
only passed ignore= when recursing into sub-directories, meaning top-level
__pycache__/, *.pyc, and similar entries would still be copied into the layer.

commit.message:
feat(contracts): architecture improvements

**Optimistic locking (PUT /api/contracts/[id])**
- Accept version (updatedAt ISO) in request body; return 409 CONFLICT if stale
- Prevents silent data loss when two users edit the same contract concurrently
- Fix race condition: findFirst now checks isDeleted:false; update where includes tenantId

**Auto-compute expiry fields on write**
- Whenever expirationDate is updated, recalculate daysUntilExpiry, isExpired,
  and expirationRisk (EXPIRED/CRITICAL ≤7d/HIGH ≤30d/MEDIUM ≤90d/LOW)
- Eliminates stale daysUntilExpiry values from stored-at-upload timestamps

**Audit log on update and delete**
- PUT: logs CONTRACT_UPDATED with diff of changed fields
- DELETE: logs CONTRACT_DELETED with deletedAt timestamp
- Both non-blocking (catch + log) so audit failure never breaks the operation

**Cursor-based pagination (GET /api/contracts)**
- Add cursor= param (base64 JSON {createdAt, id}) for keyset pagination
- Keyset avoids OFFSET performance degradation on large datasets
- Returns nextCursor in pagination envelope when more pages exist
- Falls back to offset when sortBy != createdAt or cursor absent
- Fully backward-compatible: page/limit still work as before

- Add diff_from_snapshot() export: diffs committed snapshot JSON against
  current ALS file, skipping HEAD blob download on the fast path
- Add generateCommitMessage() export: ports buildCommitMessage() logic
  from JS to Rust, removing the last JS function that read DiffReport data
- Convert all NAPI exports to async via tokio::task::spawn_blocking,
  keeping the Electron main process unblocked during ALS parsing
- Add schema_version field to Project struct with serde default for
  backwards-compatible snapshot deserialization
- Rewrite project.ts from ~800 lines to 61: remove all fast-xml-parser-
  based XML parsing, decompression, structuralCompareAls, buildLocalDiffFromAls,
  getAlsContent, and related helpers; retain only gitBin + git shell commands
- Replace get-als-content and no-commits fallback handlers with parseAls
- Remove buildCommitMessage() from main.ts
- Delete 9 orphaned types from types/index.ts (DecompressedAls, AlsChange,
  StructuralCompareResult, AudioTrackEntry, MidiTrackEntry, etc.)
- Uninstall fast-xml-parser (removes 68 packages)

JS is now purely transport: paths and buffers only. All ALS semantics
live in the Rust crate. One parser to update if Ableton's format changes.

* chore(desktop): remove old dist/vendor parser, restore git binary

* Fix build and ordering issue by implementing sorting the hashmap

* Remove outdated PIPELINE Claude file.

* Update apps/desktop/native/semantic-diff/src/diff.rs

Fix issue of unexpected characters when overflowing integers.
